text.content:
Motor blend is the secret ingredient of active ragdoll physics. It controls the transition between two extremes: fully controlled animation and fully passive ragdoll.

At blend = 1.0, every joint motor fires at maximum stiffness. The figure holds its target pose rigidly, resisting gravity and external forces. This looks like traditional animation — precise, controlled, predictable.

At blend = 0.0, all motors are disabled. The figure becomes a true ragdoll, each limb free to swing under gravity and momentum. This is what makes ragdoll physics look so natural — the emergent behavior of interconnected rigid bodies.

The magic happens in between. A blend of 0.5 creates a figure that tries to hold its pose but can be knocked around. A blend of 0.3 gives a drunk, wobbly character that barely maintains balance. Each value tells a different story.

Pose targeting adds another dimension. Instead of tracking a single standing pose, motors can target any configuration of joint angles. A pointing pose extends one arm forward. A victory pose raises both arms. The same motor blend system smoothly transitions between any pair of poses.

This is declarative physics animation. The scene JSON specifies what should happen — 'hold pose: pointing at blend 1.0' — and the physics engine figures out how to make it happen. No hand-crafted keyframes, no animation curves. Just target states and physical constraints.
